from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase (more concise for common predicates)
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "was started in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "has a height of",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is located in the",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "leader is",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "postal code is",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject to avoid repeating the subject in every clause.
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    # Build a quick lookup for location‑related information of any entity
    entity_info: dict[str, dict[str, str]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.predicate in {"location", "country", "isPartOf"}:
            entity_info.setdefault(t.subject, {})[t.predicate] = t.object

    # Helper to recursively collect full location chain for an entity
    def collect_location_chain(entity: str) -> list[str]:
        parts: list[str] = [clean_obj(entity)]
        info = entity_info.get(entity, {})
        # If the entity itself has a location, recurse first (e.g., city within state)
        if "location" in info:
            parts.extend(collect_location_chain(info["location"]))
        # Then add isPartOf and country if present
        if "isPartOf" in info:
            parts.append(clean_obj(info["isPartOf"]))
        if "country" in info:
            parts.append(clean_obj(info["country"]))
        return parts

    # Pre‑compute predicate‑object maps for every subject
    subj_to_pred_objs: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = {}
    for subj, group in subject_groups.items():
        d: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
        for tr in group:
            d.setdefault(tr.predicate, []).append(tr.object)
        subj_to_pred_objs[subj] = d

    processed: set[str] = set()
    sentences: list[str] = []

    # Helper to clean quoted objects
    def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        return o.strip('"').strip("'")

    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        if subject in processed:
            continue

        pred_objs = subj_to_pred_objs[subject]

        parts: list[str] = []
        handled: set[str] = set()

        # location chain (location, isPartOf, country)
        if any(p in pred_objs for p in ("location", "isPartOf", "country")):
            loc_chain = collect_location_chain(subject)
            # drop the subject itself (first element)
            if len(loc_chain) > 1:
                location_phrase = "located in " + ", ".join(loc_chain[1:])
                parts.append(location_phrase)
                handled.update({"location", "isPartOf", "country"})

        # building type
        if "buildingType" in pred_objs:
            btype = clean_obj(pred_objs["buildingType"][0])
            parts.append(f"is a {btype}")
            handled.add("buildingType")

        # floor count
        if "floorCount" in pred_objs:
            parts.append(f"has {clean_obj(pred_objs['floorCount'][0])} floors")
            handled.add("floorCount")

        # height
        if "height" in pred_objs:
            def normalize_height(val: str) -> str:
                v = clean_obj(val)
                if "(" in v:
                    v = v.split("(")[0].strip()
                if not any(v.lower().endswith(s) for s in ("metre", "metres", "m")):
                    v = f"{v} metres"
                return v

            heights = ", ".join(normalize_height(o) for o in pred_objs["height"])
            parts.append(f"has a height of {heights}")

    final_text = " ".join(sentences)
    if final_text:
        final_text = final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:]
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
